key_insight: MRI radiomics shows moderate ability to predict KRAS mutation status in rectal cancer from pre-treatment scans.
clinical_relevance: This could support noninvasive preoperative molecular stratification, but it is not yet accurate enough to replace molecular testing.
method: Systematic review and meta-analysis of diagnostic accuracy studies
Included 7 studies with 1,224 rectal cancer patients
Compared MRI-based radiomics/AI predictions against molecular KRAS testing
evidence_signal: Pooled performance was moderate, with sensitivity 0.736, specificity 0.645, and hierarchical summary AUC 0.754.
watch_item: Clinical use is limited by between-study heterogeneity, variable model quality, and the need for external validation before routine implementation.
paper_title: Diagnostic Accuracy of MRI Radiomics for Predicting KRAS Mutation in Rectal Cancer: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis
